genre_preference.value: sci-fi, western, drama, samurai, comic book
evidence: I usually love Samurai movies, but this isn't really a Samurai movie.
The Orville started off as a Sci-Fi comedy.
Kurosawa's story of a murder told from four different points of view that come across with distinctly different details is captivating.
The plot is deceptively simple. Kurosawa had a knack for using simple plots that he fleshed out which character development and interesting turns of events.
The movie slows down a little past halfway, and becomes very dramatic.
The picture quality on the blu ray is absolutely stunning.
The quality of the video never detracts or distracts from the movie.
Kurosawa was a filmmaking genius, and this was one of his finest films.
The Orville is a wildly entertaining show.
The special effects in Shin Godzilla are arguably even better than in the Legendary Productions 2014 Godzilla movie.
The cinematography is beautiful - this is one of the best looking Westerns ever filmed.
The movie is played straight for the most part.
The characters are often out to cover their butts to make sure they don't do anything that would get them in any kind of trouble.

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex narratives and high production quality, particularly in genres like sci-fi, westerns, and samurai films. They appreciate emotional depth and character development, while also showing a notable loyalty to directors like Akira Kurosawa.
